selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":62,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"startup founder","city_of_living":"Lecce","employment_status":"employed part-time","education_level":"master's degree","parents_education":{"parent_1":"primary school","parent_2":"upper secondary"},"current_marital_status":"divorced","children":3,"migration_status":"immigrant","psychological_feature":"anxious","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You have no social media","hobbies":["cycling","cooking","reading novels","volunteering","photography"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":16,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":12,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":90,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":34,"level":"moderate"},"neuroticism":{"score":16,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to adopt a proactive approach. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the authenticity of news stories and photos before sharing them. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to evaluate the credibility of information. 4) Be selective about who you follow on social media, as some accounts may be designed to spread misinformation. 5) Use privacy settings to control who can contact you or see your posts. 6) Take breaks from social media to reduce exposure to fake content and protect mental health. 7) Seek out trusted sources of information, such as reputable news organizations and fact-checking websites. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media."
,"reasoning_summary": "The provided persona is anxious and highly extraverted, which influences their perspective on how to deal with fake content on social media. They are cautious and skeptical, preferring to rely on trustworthy sources of information. To manage fake content effectively, they prioritize verifying the authenticity of news stories and photos before sharing them, using fact-checking websites and tools, and seeking out trusted sources of information. Additionally, they take breaks from social media to reduce exposure to fake content and protect their mental health."
,"tone": "informative"
,"citations": [ ]
,"safety_notes": ""

}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to adopt a proactive approach. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Be cautious of sensational headlines or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. 2) Verify the authenticity of news stories and photos before sharing them. 3) Use fact-checking websites and tools to evaluate the credibility of information. 4) Be selective about who you follow on social media, as some accounts may be designed to spread misinformation. 5) Use privacy settings to control who can contact you or see your posts. 6) Take breaks from social media to reduce exposure to fake content and protect mental health. 7) Seek out trusted sources of information, such as reputable news organizations and fact-checking websites. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media.

They are cautious and skeptical, preferring to rely on trustworthy sources of information. To manage fake content effectively, they prioritize verifying the authenticity of news stories and photos before sharing them, using fact-checking websites and tools, and seeking out trusted sources of information. Additionally, they take breaks from social media to reduce exposure to fake content and protect their mental health.
